q: The environmental problems directly related to energy production and consumption include air pollution, climate change, water pollution, ___ pollution, and solid waste disposal.
Global warming refers to the gradual increase in the average temperature of the earth's surface and its atmosphere due to the accumulation of ___ gases.
The main greenhouse gases are carbon dioxide, methane, water vapour, oxides of nitrogen, and ___.
CFCs also destroy the earth's protective ___ layer.
Acid rains are caused mainly by the release of sulphur dioxide and oxides of ___ when fossil fuels burn.
Because ___ acid is a relatively weak acid, the ability of carbon dioxide alone to generate true acid rain is very limited.
Acidified clouds could travel great ___ before releasing the acid rain.
The problems posed by acid rains include corrosion of the built environment, soil degradation, water pollution, and depletion of ___.
The oil industry adds ___ to petrol (gasoline) to help engines run more smoothly.
Because carbonic acid is a relatively weak acid, the ability of carbon dioxide alone to generate true ___ is very limited.
The problems posed by acid rains include corrosion of the built environment, soil degradation, water pollution and depletion of ___.
The oil industry adds ___ to petrol (gasoline) to help engines run more smoothly.
Vehicles that burn leaded gasoline pour out leaded fumes that contaminate the ___.
The World Health Organisation (WHO) has established that smoke from the combustion of leaded fuels in vehicles causes cancer and high ___ in adults;
and in children, it impairs mental development, reduces intelligence thus hindering learning ability and causes ___.
Oil spills seriously damage the land, vegetation, and water bodies, including the ___.
Excavated areas that have been strip mined for coal but are not filled and revegetated cause water pollution as ___ runoff from the mined area
can flush sediments and ___ compounds into nearby streams and rivers.
Photovoltaics is now a proven technology which is inherently ___, as opposed to some dangerous electricity generating technologies.
Over its estimated life a photovoltaic module will produce much more ___ than was used in its production.
A solar module will prevent the emission of ___ produced by other conventional energy sources.
PV cell technologies have relatively lower environmental ___ compared to other types of electric sources.
A wind farm, when installed on agricultural land, has one of the lowest environmental impacts of all ___.
It occupies less land area per ___ of electricity generated than any other energy conversion system.
Modern wind turbine designs have significantly reduced the ___ from turbines.
Low frequency sound and ___ (i.e. usually beneath the threshold of human hearing) are everywhere in the environment.
Wind turbines may create shadow flicker on residences when the ___ passes behind the turbine.
Although hydropower has no air quality impacts, construction and operation of hydropower dams can significantly affect natural ___ systems
in flat basins large dams cause flooding of large tracts of land, destroying local animals and ___,
large amounts of plant life are submerged and decay ___,
dams restrict sediments that are responsible for the ___ downstream,
Geothermal power is a relatively ___ source of energy.
Electricity generation from geothermal resources involves much lower ___ emission rates than that from fossil fuels.
Biomass power plants share some similarities with fossil fuel power plants: both involve the combustion of a ___ to generate electricity.
Biomass power plants, like coal and natural gas-fired power plants, require water for ___.
A ___ is a natural substance that is not replenished with the speed at which it is consumed.
___ (also called non-conventional energy sources) are continuously replenished by natural processes.
Conversion of light energy into electrical energy is based on a phenomenon called ___.
These substances can rise very high into the atmosphere, where they mix and react with water, oxygen, and other chemicals to form more acidic pollutants, known as ___.
